Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 44,450.00 moved across the accounts in this case, with Rs 34,750.00 coming from outside the suspect network.
  • The largest source of funds was gatreddy@ok, which sent out Rs 15,150.00.
  • The account 763010062770 sent money to 5 different accounts.
  • The account MR. ANUPDUBEY received money from 6 different accounts.
  • Rs 1,776,949.96 of the incoming funds were traced to onward transfers or withdrawals, while Rs 442,450.04 could not be fully traced.
  • The account KAMALESH SUBHASH INAMDAR is the first to investigate due to its suspicious behaviors.

4 · Findings by Account

KAMALESH SUBHASH INAMDAR strong
acct_003 · Unknown Bank · Account Number 1357102000000198 · Score 1,003
Rank #1

The account of KAMALESH SUBHASH INAMDAR (1357102000000198) is suspicious due to a large credit transaction of 560000.00 on 2021-03-22, which was subsequently allocated to 7 debit transactions. This pattern, combined with other findings, suggests potential money laundering activity. The account's transactions exhibit structuring and smurfing behaviors, further indicating suspicious financial activity.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit transaction of 560000.00 on 2021-03-22 was traced into 7 subsequent debit transactions, with the entire amount allocated and no remaining balance. (+7 more of this type)
  • Structuring Smurfing Detection strong — On 2020-04-28, 7 debit transactions totaling 75550.00 were made, with each transaction below the individual cutoff but collectively exceeding it. (+4 more of this type)
  • Duplicate Verification weak — Similar transactions were flagged for further review due to their similarities, with a narration similarity of 0.8709677419354839 and same reference but different counterparty. (+31 more of this type)
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph was constructed with 14 nodes and 13 directed transaction edges, indicating a complex transaction pattern with high confidence score of 1.0.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — 4 suspicious round-value debits totaling 587000.00 were made, with all debits being multiples of 1000, suggesting potential structuring activity.
Assessment: 5 strong pattern type(s) and 4 distinct pattern(s) — strong evidence.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
